lesson.menu_desc: Present your company to a foreign investor — what it does, its size and position, using superlatives.
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 — Describing Your Company: Centauro, Nike Brazil & SPF Group
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Daniela Feitoza | Aula 5 -- Describing Your Company

listenings.text: Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, listen to one short voice note about describing your company with superlatives. When you talk about your company to an investor, you often compare it to others. You say it is the biggest, the most important, or the best in the market. Here is the key. For short adjectives, add e s t. Big becomes the biggest. Large becomes the largest. Fast becomes the fastest. For long adjectives, use the most. Important becomes the most important. Successful becomes the most successful. And remember, always use the word the before a superlative. Say the largest retailer, never largest retailer. Also avoid two mistakes. Never say the most big. Never say the more largest. Now try it. Describe your company. Say what it does, where it is headquartered, and one thing it is the best at. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus from the New York office. I am leaving you a quick voicemail before our investor call tomorrow. So, let me give you a short profile of our group. SPF Group is headquartered in Sao Paulo, and it is one of the largest sportswear companies in Latin America. Centauro is our main subsidiary, and it is the market leader in sportswear retail in Brazil. We also distribute Nike products across the country, so we manage a very large supply chain. The group generates strong annual revenue and employs thousands of people. When you describe us to the investor, remember to highlight that Centauro is the biggest sportswear retailer in the region and that our distribution network is the most complete in the market. Keep it simple and confident. Call me back if you have questions. Talk soon, Daniela.
